hypothesis: Add --kv-cache-dtype fp8 to int4_mtp_bi0_surgattn to halve KV read bandwidth for free TPS at PPL<=2.42.
summary: No fp8 KV dtype is serviceable on A10G (Ampere/sm_86) for the int4-compressed-tensors + force-2D-TRITON_ATTN + torch.compile stack. Re-confirms the prior fp8-KV dead-end (wirbel #141; BASELINE.md 'rejected by A10G + Gemma4 attn') with a precise three-layer root cause.
hardware: NVIDIA A10G, Ampere sm_86, 23028 MiB, no native fp8 tensor cores
stack: vllm==0.22.0, transformers==5.9.0, target=google/gemma-4-E4B-it-qat-w4a16-ct (compressed-tensors W4A16), TRITON_ATTN backend (force-selected for Gemma4 heterogeneous head dims), torch.compile/inductor + cudagraph, MTP drafter K=6, VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=0

three_layer_block.name: vLLM init guard (fp8_e5m2)
three_layer_block.mechanism: CompressedTensorsConfig.get_quant_method returns CompressedTensorsKVCacheMethod for EVERY Attention layer (compressed_tensors.py:181-182), so should_load_quant_weights=True even though the W4A16 checkpoint has kv_cache_scheme=None; the guard then raises ValueError: 'fp8_e5m2 kv-cache is not supported with fp8 checkpoints'.
three_layer_block.status: RELAXED by submissions/int4_mtp_bi0_fp8kv/vllm_fp8kv_e5m2_guard_patch.py (only when kv_cache_scheme is None). This is a legitimate narrowing of an over-broad guard; it gets e5m2 past layer 1 but layers 2-3 still block.

three_layer_block.name: attention-forward dtype assert (rejects e5m2)
three_layer_block.mechanism: When the fp8 KV path activates query_quant, the forward asserts the dtype is e4m3/nvfp4-class. fp8_e5m2 is not in the set -> torch._dynamo.exc.Unsupported: Data-dependent assertion failed (cannot compile partial graph). The kernel implements only e4m3/nvfp4 dequant. This is BASELINE.md's 'rejected by Gemma4 attn'. Not patchable without rewriting the attention impl.

three_layer_block.name: Triton sm_86 fp8 compile
three_layer_block.site: inductor autotuning of fused KV-store/RMSNorm kernel (triton_per_fused__to_copy_..._rms_norm_...)
three_layer_block.error: ValueError("type fp8e4nv not supported in this architecture. The supported fp8 dtypes are ('fp8e4b15', 'fp8e5')")
three_layer_block.mechanism: fp8_e4m3 (and bare 'fp8' which aliases to fp8_e4m3 per torch_utils.py:64) maps to Triton fp8e4nv, unsupported on sm_86. This is BASELINE.md's 'rejected by A10G'.

exhaustive_dtype_table.fp8_e5m2: past layer1 (patched) -> DEAD at layer2 (kernel assert). Would compile in Triton (fp8e5) if it reached layer3.
exhaustive_dtype_table.fp8_e4m3: ok layer1, ok layer2 -> DEAD at layer3 (Triton fp8e4nv unsupported on sm_86)
exhaustive_dtype_table.fp8: aliases to fp8_e4m3 -> DEAD at layer3
exhaustive_dtype_table.int8: NOT a valid vLLM CacheDType literal (only int8_per_token_head exists, a different unsupported path)

key_fact: The two Ampere-compilable Triton fp8 dtypes (fp8e4b15, fp8e5) are exactly the two the vLLM attention forward does NOT accept; the two it accepts (fp8_e4m3, nvfp4) require Ada/Hopper/Blackwell. No fp8 dtype clears all three layers.
shipped_config: KV_CACHE_DTYPE=auto => default bf16 KV == byte-for-byte int4_mtp_bi0_surgattn (the only config that boots on A10G).

fp8kv_measured.note: Not measurable: no fp8 KV config boots on A10G.

evidence_logs: research/validity/bi0_fp8kv/_smoke_e5m2.out (+ _smoke_e5m2_server.log): guard relaxed (layer1 passed), layer-2 kernel assert kv_cache_dtype in {fp8,fp8_e4m3,nvfp4}
recommendation: Close the fp8-KV lever for sm_86 (already in BASELINE.md confirmed-dead-ends). A bootable fp8 path would require either (a) a new sm_86 fp8 attention kernel that dequants e5m2 (none in vLLM 0.22.0), or (b) Ada/Hopper/Blackwell hardware. Neither is in scope for this challenge's A10G target.
